content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
September 7, L990
Manuel G. Nunez
4983 E. Grant
Fresno, CA 93727
Re: Your Request For Advice
our File No. A-90-522
Dear Ur. Nunez:
This is in response to your fetter of August 4, L99o,
reguesting confirnation of telephone advice concerning the
canpaign financing provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
rActrr ) . r
OUESTION
you have inquired whether you, as an elected Trustee of the
Fresno unified school Board, may use your campaign funds to pay
for the cost of defending a case in which your election is
contested.
CONCLUSION
The Act does not prohibit the use of an elected official's
carnpaign funds for the cost of defending a case in which that
official's 
election is contested. However, because other Iegal
provisions could inpact on that situation, you should contact your
board or county counsel as to those matters.
FACTS
on June 5, L99o, you were elected to the position of Fresno
Unified School District Trustee. An unsuccessful candidate in
your area is now contestingT the election.
1' 
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. A11 statutory references
are to the Goverrunent Code unLess otherwise indicated. 
Commission
regulations appear at 2 california code of Regulations section
18000, et seq. Al-1 references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 8OO o P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 95804-0807 o (9f 6)322-5660


ANALYS I S
section 85202 provides that campaj'gn funds are held in trust
for expenses associated with the election of the candidate.
artictE 8 (Section 85800 - 85802) specifies the linitations 
on the
use of those funds. Generally, funds may be used by an elected
officer for purPoses reasonably related to a legislative or
governnental purpose. (Section I5e 01)
Section 85802.5 provides thats
rt(a) Expenditures of campaign funds for attorney's
fees and'costs in connection with ... litigation 
are not
directly related to a politicaf, 
legislative, 
or
governlTrantal purpose except where the litigation 
arises
directly out of a(n) ... elected officer's 
... status as
a(n) ... elected officer, including, ... an action
arising from an election contest or recount.tl
IEmphasis added. ]
Thus, the use of carnpaign funds for attorney fees and other
litigation 
expenses in the defense of your status as Trustee is
not prohibited by the Pol,itical Reform Act.
Because the advice provided by the Fair Pol-iticaL Practices
Cornnission is limited to the provisions of the Act, other legal
provisions, such as any local- ordinances which nay have some
Learing on the situation, are beyond the scope of this.letter'
You sh6u1d contact the school board's attorney for advice as to
these matters.
I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
reguested. ff you have any further questions regarding this nat-
ter, please contact ne at (916) 322-59OL.
Our Fi■e No.
Page 2
SH:」G:dg
A-90-522
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting ceneral counsel
Divi s ion
